ucs.title: [LLM] APT1 (Comment Crew / Unit 61398) email staging via password-protected RAR archives of PST/OST stores
ucs.description: Hunts the signature APT1 collection-then-exfil chain documented in the Mandiant APT1 report: their GETMAIL/MAPIGET tooling harvests Outlook PST/OST or .msg files, which are then staged into password-protected RAR archives (often named with single-letter or date-based filenames) prior to exfiltration. The combination of touching a local mail store and creating an encrypted RAR within the same host/user session is highly characteristic of Comment Crew tradecraft.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_process_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe") AND (Processes.process="*-hp*" OR Processes.process="*-p\"*" OR Processes.process=" -p *" OR Processes.process="* a *")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_id _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest user [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name IN ("*.pst","*.ost","*.msg","*.eml") OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Outlook\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_id _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename count as mail_file_touch_count | where mail_file_touch_count >= 1] | where lastTime - firstTime <= 3600 | eval description="APT1 email-archive staging: password-protected RAR created in same session that touched local mail store" | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name process mail_file_touch_count description
ucs.kql:
let window = 1h;
let archivers = dynamic(["rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe"]);
let ArchiveEvents = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ (archivers)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (" -hp", " -p\"", " -p ", " a ")
| project ArchiveTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ArchiveCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ArchiveProc=FileName, ArchiveParent=InitiatingProcessFileName;
let MailFileEvents = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName endswith ".pst" or FileName endswith ".ost" or FileName endswith ".msg" or FileName endswith ".eml"
   or FolderPath has @"\Microsoft\Outlook\"
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| project MailTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName, MailFile=FileName, MailFolder=FolderPath, MailProc=InitiatingProcessFileName;
ArchiveEvents
| join kind=inner (MailFileEvents) on DeviceId, AccountName
| where abs(datetime_diff('second', ArchiveTime, MailTime)) <= 3600
| summarize MailFiles=make_set(MailFile, 25), MailProcs=make_set(MailProc, 10), FirstMail=min(MailTime), LastArchive=max(ArchiveTime) by DeviceName, AccountName, ArchiveCmd, ArchiveParent
| extend Detection="APT1/Comment Crew GETMAIL+RAR exfil staging pattern"
| order by LastArchive desc
ucs.rationale: The Mandiant APT1 report explicitly documents Unit 61398's tooling (GETMAIL, MAPIGET) and their habitual use of RAR with the -hp (header+payload encryption) flag to package stolen email before exfil. Generic ransomware crews compress data, but few legitimate enterprise workflows touch PST/OST and produce a password-encrypted RAR within the same user session on the same host. ucs.title: [LLM] APT1 cmd.exe post-exploitation recon burst (Mandiant APT1 'standard hello' enumeration sequence)
ucs.description: Detects the canonical APT1 post-access reconnaissance burst: within minutes of landing on a host, operators spawn cmd.exe and run a tightly-clustered sequence of enumeration commands (ipconfig /all, net view, net view /domain, net group "domain admins" /domain, net localgroup administrators, tasklist /v, netstat -ano, systeminfo, net use). Mandiant's APT1 report calls this the operator's 'hello' to a new host. The chain is what fingerprints the actor — any 4+ of these from one cmd.exe parent inside a 10-minute window is highly indicative of APT1 / Comment Crew hands-on-keyboard.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as commands min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_procs from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="cmd.exe" AND (Processes.process_name IN ("ipconfig.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","systeminfo.exe","whoami.exe","nbtstat.exe","hostname.exe","query.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_id _span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval recon_hits=mvcount(commands) | where recon_hits >= 4 AND (lastTime - firstTime) <= 600 | eval has_netview=if(match(mvjoin(commands,"|"),"net(1)?\.exe\s+view"),1,0), has_domadmins=if(match(mvjoin(commands,"|"),"domain admins"),1,0), has_ipconfig=if(match(mvjoin(commands,"|"),"ipconfig.*\/all"),1,0), has_tasklist=if(match(mvjoin(commands,"|"),"tasklist.*\/v"),1,0), has_netstat=if(match(mvjoin(commands,"|"),"netstat.*-ano"),1,0) | eval apt1_signature_score=has_netview+has_domadmins+has_ipconfig+has_tasklist+has_netstat | where apt1_signature_score >= 3 | eval description="Probable APT1 hands-on-keyboard recon burst" | table firstTime lastTime dest user recon_hits apt1_signature_score commands description
ucs.kql:
let window = 10m;
let ReconBins =
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "cmd.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("ipconfig.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","systeminfo.exe","whoami.exe","nbtstat.exe","hostname.exe","query.exe")
| extend Bin=bin(Timestamp, window)
| extend
    has_netview = iff(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\bnet1?\.exe\s+view\b", 1, 0),
    has_domadmins = iff(ProcessCommandLine has "domain admins", 1, 0),
    has_ipconfig_all = iff(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)ipconfig.*\/all", 1, 0),
    has_tasklist_v = iff(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)tasklist.*\/v", 1, 0),
    has_netstat_ano = iff(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)netstat.*-ano", 1, 0),
    has_netuse = iff(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\bnet1?\.exe\s+use\b", 1, 0),
    has_localgrp = iff(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)localgroup\s+administrators", 1, 0)
| summarize
    ReconCmds = make_list(ProcessCommandLine, 25),
    DistinctTools = dcount(FileName),
    APT1Score = sum(has_netview)+sum(has_domadmins)+sum(has_ipconfig_all)+sum(has_tasklist_v)+sum(has_netstat_ano)+sum(has_netuse)+sum(has_localgrp),
    FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
    LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
    ParentCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
    Grandparent = any(InitiatingProcessParentFileName)
  by DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessId, Bin
| where DistinctTools >= 4 and APT1Score >= 3;
ReconBins
| extend Detection = "APT1/Comment Crew operator hello sequence (cmd.exe recon burst)"
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, DeviceName, AccountName, Grandparent, ParentCmd, DistinctTools, APT1Score, ReconCmds, Detection
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Mandiant's APT1 report (and follow-on FireEye/CrowdStrike reporting on Comment Panda) repeatedly highlights this exact enumeration set as the operator's first action after gaining access — they are creatures of habit running the same recipe by hand from cmd.exe. The signature-score gate (must include domain admins enumeration OR net view OR netstat -ano combos) separates this from a sysadmin runni
